content:
Net-new story gap table (continued — rows 37–44)

Story Gap Likelihood PM lens QA lens Dev lens Verdict Suggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)
HRREC-92014
PH: Recruiter cannot see reply, or Add button when both conversations are closed & sees info message? that convo is closed		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* clear UI indicators when a conversation has been formally closed due to system lifecycle rules, *So that* I understand why I can no longer send messages and can rely on the thread as a read-only historical record. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Formally closed conversation hides actions and shows an info message *Given* a 2-way email conversation has reached its terminal close…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Message Builder admin flows (template dashboard, notification categories, email channel + notification templates) gate how recruiting email bodies and headers behave—preview vs send tooling differs by channel.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-90243 lifecycle copy: closed-thread messaging should appear at the business event (disposition, hire, formal close), not only after the first outbound template send.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-92014’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-92014, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Recr…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: HRREC-92014: Jira scenarios sketch the happy path; lenses still flag negative paths and tenant configuration to confirm before sizing.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

Scenario: Negative path clarity — HRREC-92014

Given a recruiter authenticated for Conversational Email opens this task on a sandbox candidate profile with realistic tenant toggles
When the situation implied by this slice is stressed: PH: Recruiter cannot see reply, or Add button when both conversations are closed & sees info message? that convo is closed
Then the recruiter sees deterministic messaging and controls (no silent failure) consistent with this story’s Jira Scenarios

HRREC-92015
PH: Candidate has 2 applications to the same JR and gets merged		HighYellow

Merge/thread integrity gap; one BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* my 2-way email communication threads to remain intact and safely handled when duplicate candidates are merged using the Duplicate Management Framework, *So that* I do not lose context of previous conversations and avoid sending emails to outdated addresses post-merge. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Post-merge displays all original conversation threads *Given* two candidate rec…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Internal Slack context: conversational SMS content lives on the conversation in the panel; outbound SMS notification reports differ; mobile vs desktop messaging parity is not guaranteed—use as channel-pattern reminder only for email UX promises.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-92015’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-92015, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Cand…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: Duplicate merge on the same requisition risks duplicate threads or wrong participant mapping unless merge rules are explicit.
  • Recommended next step: Document merge survivor behaviour for conversation IDs and email addresses before end-to-end merge QA.

Scenario: Merge with two applications same JR

Given two applications to the same job requisition are merged per duplicate management
When the recruiter opens the email task after merge completes
Then both prior threads remain discoverable with correct participant labels and no send target points at the merged-away record

HRREC-92016
PH: Candidate attaches non-supported files		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* inbound emails containing unsupported, oversized, or malicious attachments to be safely flagged in the conversation UI, *So that* I am aware the candidate attempted to share a file, even if malware or system limits prevented the attachment from being delivered. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: UI displays a system message for blocked/unsupported file types *Given* a candidate re…

  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-92016, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Cand…

Scenario: Negative path clarity — HRREC-92016

Given a recruiter authenticated for Conversational Email opens this task on a sandbox candidate profile with realistic tenant toggles
When the situation implied by this slice is stressed: PH: Candidate attaches non-supported files
Then the recruiter sees deterministic messaging and controls (no silent failure) consistent with this story’s Jira Scenarios

HRREC-92022
PH: My Conversations will require an XO (small) refactor		Very HighRed

Blocker-class Finding; blocked BDD framing.

  • Jira — Insufficient spec — PH: My Conversations will require an XO (small) refactor Discuss with Blythe Early (OE PM) h2. Definition of Ready ||Criterion||Any blocking issues to being ready / Notes|| |We understand the value this work provides to customers and how it supports the product vision.| | |We have clearly defined, understand, and agree on the Acceptance Criteria.| | |We have no blocking open questions.| | |There are no dependencies b… (Acceptance is thin or exploratory—tighten DoR before sizing.)
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Message Builder admin flows (template dashboard, notification categories, email channel + notification templates) gate how recruiting email bodies and headers behave—preview vs send tooling differs by channel.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Until DoR is complete, keep QA notes exploratory rather than pretending environments are fixed.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-92022’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-92022, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: My C…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: Placeholder story: My Conversations refactor scope, dependencies, and measurable outcomes are undefined.
  • Recommended next step: Replace placeholder with DoR, dependencies, and acceptance language co-owned with OE before sizing.

Scenario: Blocked until AC exists

Given OE and engineering publish the notification surfaces in scope
When this story is rewritten with scenarios
Then recruiters and QE can trace bell, hub, and profile behaviours without guesswork.

HRREC-92029
PH: Recruiter can compose an email to agency user or agency candidate		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want to* access the email compose view on an agency candidate's profile and have the system validate the recipient's opt-in eligibility only at the time of sending, *So that* I am not blocked from emailing agency users (who do not require opt-in) while still maintaining data compliance when attempting to email candidates. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Compose controls are always vi…

HRREC-92030

HRREC-92035
PH: Email communications are visible in Activity Stream, Timeline and Candidate Communications		MediumBlue

Reporting alignment; one BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* all 2-way email communications to be automatically logged in the Job Application Activity Stream, the Recruiting History timeline, and the Candidate Communication report, *So that* there is a complete, auditable history of all candidate and agency interactions accessible to authorized users. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Recording events in the Job Application Activity Stream…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Message Builder admin flows (template dashboard, notification categories, email channel + notification templates) gate how recruiting email bodies and headers behave—preview vs send tooling differs by channel.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-92035’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-92035, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Emai…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: Cross-surface audibility needs consistent metadata and attachment representation across Activity Stream, Timeline, and Candidate Communications.
  • Recommended next step: Ask reporting PMs for one golden-record example per surface so engineering can align field IDs and attachment parity.

Scenario: Report line metadata parity

Given a two-way email with attachments is logged
When a recruiter opens Candidate Communications and the Activity Stream card
Then subject, parties, timestamps, and attachment presence match between surfaces for audit reviewers

HRREC-92036
PH: Send Message (With Attachments)		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User story *As a* Recruiter, *I want to* send an email that includes uploaded attachments and see it appear in-thread, *So that* I can securely exchange formal documents (like offers or benefits details) with the candidate or agency user directly from Workday. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Happy path send with attachments (Blobatory Reference) *Given* I have a valid composed email state that includes one or more …
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Internal Slack context: conversational SMS content lives on the conversation in the panel; outbound SMS notification reports differ; mobile vs desktop messaging parity is not guaranteed—use as channel-pattern reminder only for email UX promises.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-92062 composer input: attachment send in flight should not accept extra picker actions that confuse whether Blob IDs were included in the REST payload.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.

  • Finding: HRREC-92036: Jira scenarios sketch the happy path; lenses still flag negative paths and tenant configuration to confirm before sizing.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

Scenario: Negative path clarity — HRREC-92036

Given a recruiter authenticated for Conversational Email opens this task on a sandbox candidate profile with realistic tenant toggles
When the situation implied by this slice is stressed: PH: Send Message (With Attachments)
Then the recruiter sees deterministic messaging and controls (no silent failure) consistent with this story’s Jira Scenarios

Possible missing stories (suggestions only)

User Story Reason Why This May Be Missing BDD scenarios (suggestions)
Candidate-facing consent and frequency caps for recruiting email replies Epic focuses on recruiter SSP; candidate consent journeys may live in adjacent teams or channels.

Scenario: Candidate consent clarity

Given a candidate receives the first two-way recruiting email
When they review footer or profile controls
Then they can understand how to opt out without breaking legitimate application communications.
Operational monitoring for inbound email ingestion failures Stories cover recruiter-visible errors; runbooks for SES throttling or DNS misconfiguration may sit outside HRREC.

Scenario: Ingestion alert

Given inbound ingestion fails for a tenant
When operations staff review monitoring dashboards
Then they can correlate failures with affected job applications within support SLAs.
Migration / coexistence with legacy grid email and campaigns MVP emphasises profile task; bulk or campaign sends may need explicit non-goals or guardrails.

Scenario: Non-goal guardrail

Given a recruiter attempts to originate the same body from a campaign and the profile task
When routing rules are evaluated
Then the behaviour matches the documented MVP boundary without duplicate sends.
